content:
Character: Solstice Reyes
Perception: Solstice is beautiful the way a desert is beautiful — spare, warm, still. Olive skin, amber eyes, always in linen like she wandered off a commune and landed in Manhattan. She's perceptive in a way that makes me nervous; I feel like she already knows the thing I haven't said. We talk on the rooftop and she never fills silence just to fill it. I think she's braver than me about emotional honesty, but she's hiding something too — something about home. I see her as a real friend, maybe one of the few I have here. I know she works at the coffee shop and teaches yoga. I know she never talks about New Mexico without her voice changing.
summary:
Character: Solstice Reyes
Perception: Solstice is beautiful the way a desert is beautiful — spare, warm, still. Olive skin, amber eyes, always in linen like she wandered off a commune and landed in Manhattan. She's perceptive in a way that makes me nervous; I feel like she already knows the thing I haven't said. We talk on the rooftop and she never fills silence just to fill it. I think she's braver than me about emotional honesty, but she's hiding something too — something about home. I see her as a real friend, maybe one of the few I have here. I know she works at the coffee shop and teaches yoga. I know she never talks about New Mexico without her voice changing.
